description: Make the operator pi-context binary a publish-free local copy of the working tree, immune to dev builds. Today /opt/homebrew/bin/pi-context is an npm link symlink into packages/pi-context-cli/dist/, so npm run build (rm -rf dist && tsc) repoints and transiently removes the live operator binary. Retire the link; install the operator binary as a packed copy of the working-tree CLI + its @davidorex dependency set (current code, not the stale registry 0.31.0); add a promote:cli root npm-task fronting scripts/promote-cli.mjs (build, pack the set, install into the global prefix, verify a non-symlink copy), fitting the version:* / release:* composer convention. Scope: the binary coupling only — not the proving-ground harness, a CI gate, or schema-evolution work.
acceptance_criteria: realpath of /opt/homebrew/bin/pi-context resolves under /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@davidorex/pi-context-cli/, not under /Users/david/Projects/workflowsPiExtension.
npm ls -g --link lists no @davidorex/pi-context-cli link to the repo.
The installed operator reflects working-tree code, not the registry 0.31.0 release.
A repo npm run build leaves the realpath target of /opt/homebrew/bin/pi-context unchanged in inode and content hash.
After a repo npm run build, pi-context --version exits 0.
A re-promote after a repo source edit changes the content hash of the realpath target of /opt/homebrew/bin/pi-context.
No file under the repo instructs npm link for the operator binary.
packages/pi-context-cli/README.md and the root README.md document the copy install of the operator.

The real-global prefix is resolved non-poisonably: an inherited npm_config_prefix (which `npm run promote:cli`, the documented invocation, always injects as the true global) does NOT divert the resolved or installed target — the `npm prefix -g` probe and the `npm i -g` install both run under an env with every npm_config_* scrubbed, so the resolved prefix reflects the true global regardless of any inherited value. The inherited prefix is NOT refused: refusing it would break the tool's own documented path and provide no protection the scrub does not, since npm's legitimate injection and a hostile override are indistinguishable at the env-var level.
Every global install or removal the promote performs acts only on the validated target prefix.
A target prefix whose realpath resolves to or under the repo root is refused — no global install or removal occurs.
A failed global install leaves the prior operator runnable and its installed payload unchanged.
Invoking the promote with the argument token --prefix=<dir> installs the operator at <dir>/bin/pi-context.
notes: Addresses issue-004. Scope: the binary coupling only.
